critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
The 6% watch-time lift is confounded by: (1) a major creator campaign launching week 2 of the rollout, providing an independent high-engagement content stimulus; (2) pooling of notification-driven and home-feed sessions; (3) no log replay counterfactual. The new ranker may be better on ordinary home-feed traffic, but the aggregate metric cannot establish this. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
The new model has legitimate design improvements (larger candidate pool, new loss function). Stable ad load is one positive control. However, the promotion campaign and entrypoint mixing are genuine confounders that prevent attributing the 6% lift to feed ranking quality. An entrypoint-separated or log-replay analysis is needed. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Both assessors agree: aggregate lift is confounded; model may still be better on home-feed ordinary traffic; empirical test needed.

SYNTHESIS:
Consensus on all three issues. The new ranker may genuinely improve home-feed engagement; the current evidence cannot support the attribution claim.

ETD SPECIFICATION: The key contested claim is whether the new feed ranker improves engagement on ordinary home-feed requests independent of the promotion campaign.
- Measure: Average watch-time per session for home-feed-originated sessions only, excluding the two weeks of event-promotion traffic; log replay with both rankers on the same sample of home-feed requests from the pre-campaign rollout period
- Success criterion: After removing event-promotion weeks and restricting to home-feed sessions, the new ranker shows no material improvement (e.g., under 1.5%) — confirming the aggregate 6% was promotion and notification driven
- Failure criterion: New ranker shows consistent watch-time improvement on home-feed non-event sessions in log replay (e.g., 3%+ on matched requests) — confirming genuine feed ranking improvement independent of the promotional campaign

empirical_test.measure: Watch-time per session for home-feed-originated, non-event-promotion-week sessions; log replay of both rankers on matched home-feed requests
empirical_test.success_criterion: New ranker shows no material improvement on home-feed non-event sessions — confirming aggregate lift was promotion/notification driven
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New ranker shows consistent watch-time improvement on matched home-feed non-event sessions — confirming genuine ranking improvement
